text.title: langchain-basics Core Rules
text.summary: Six testable rules for LangChain basics.

rule.statement: Use `model.with_structured_output(PydanticModel)` over `JsonOutputParser` for any chain whose downstream code depends on specific fields; function calling is more reliable than prompt-based JSON extraction.
rule.rationale: `JsonOutputParser` fails silently on malformed JSON; smaller models drift on prompt-based formats. Function calling enforces the schema at the API level.

rule.statement: `langchain`, `langchain-core`, and provider packages MUST be pinned to exact versions in `requirements.txt`; minor version bumps routinely change interfaces.
rule.rationale: LangChain 0.1 → 0.2 → 0.3 introduced breaking changes; unpinned environments break unpredictably.

rule.statement: `LANGCHAIN_TRACING_V2=true` MUST be set in development environments; disable in production only if tracing latency matters.
rule.rationale: Traces are invaluable for debugging prompt failures and understanding execution order.

rule.statement: Every chain calling an external API MUST use `chain.with_retry(stop_after_attempt=3, wait_exponential_jitter=True)` to handle transient network errors.
rule.rationale: Transient errors are common; explicit retry is required for production reliability.

rule.statement: User-facing chain outputs MUST use `chain.astream()`; streaming reduces perceived latency significantly even when total tokens are unchanged.
rule.rationale: Users tolerate longer total time when they see progress; non-streaming chains feel broken.

rule.statement: Chains MUST be defined at module level, NOT inside request handlers; chain compilation happens once at import time.
rule.rationale: Recreating chains per request wastes CPU and prevents reuse across contexts.
